maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found. Wyoming statutes do not set a maximum contract term for a public school district's contract with a private student-transportation / school-bus contractor. The general school-district procurement bidding statute, W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii), addresses the procedural threshold for competitive bidding and the procurement of 'insurance, supplies or materials other than textbooks' and capital construction projects; it does not cap the term of services contracts. The student-transportation funding statute, W.S. § 21-13-320, governs state reimbursement of district transportation maintenance-and-operation expenditures and establishes a state-level competitive-bidding base-price process for the acquisition of school buses and other student-transportation vehicles, but does not regulate the term of contracts between districts and private transportation contractors. No transportation-specific term cap was located in Title 21 (Education); a check of Title 16 chapter 6 (Public Property — Public Works and Contracts, Articles 1-10) confirmed that its articles address public-works construction, resident-labor and Wyoming-materials preferences, public-printing contracts, life-cycle cost analyses, accessibility, agency-account payments, alternate delivery methods for construction, works of art, and capital construction restrictions — none of which sets a maximum term for a school district's services contract.
maxContractTerm.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-110(a)(viii); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-320; Wyo. Stat. Ann. tit. 16 ch. 6 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf and https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title16.pdf)

rebidRequired.label: Conditional
rebidRequired.rule: Wyoming has no transportation-services-specific rebid statute. W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) requires a school district to '[o]btain competitive bids when any purchase of insurance, supplies or materials other than textbooks costing more than twenty-five thousand dollars ($25,000.00) and less than fifty thousand dollars ($50,000.00) is contemplated unless precluded by other regulation or statute,' and to publish a 'call for bids ... at least once in a newspaper of general circulation in the district and on the state procurement website' when the purchase 'is equal to or exceeds fifty thousand dollars ($50,000.00).' 'No contract shall be divided for the purpose of avoiding this paragraph.' The same paragraph also provides that '[a] district shall independently meet the requirements of this paragraph when procuring goods or services that are subject to this paragraph through a board of cooperative educational services' — a reference to 'goods or services' that creates textual ambiguity as to whether the bidding requirement reaches a district's contract for transportation services more broadly. The 2025 Wyoming Legislative Service Office (LSO) memo 'The Use of Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' identifies W.S. § 9-2-3006 and W.S. § 21-3-110 as the 'Conflicting Statutes' for public-entity procurement and confirms that 21-3-110(a)(viii) prescribes a 'specific, step-by-step competitive bidding process that each entity must conduct independently.' W.S. § 21-13-320(f) requires WDE to use a competitive bidding process for the state's vehicle-acquisition base-price, but does not require district rebid of transportation-services contracts at term-end. The verdict 'Conditional' reflects that a Wyoming district must run a competitive process for a transportation-services purchase only insofar as the purchase falls within 21-3-110(a)(viii)'s dollar thresholds and statutory scope (which the statute's text limits to 'insurance, supplies or materials,' with the BOCES 'goods or services' clause as an unresolved interpretive question); no Wyoming statute mandates rebidding at the end of a transportation-services contract term as a transportation-specific rule.
rebidRequired.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-110(a)(viii); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-320(f); LSO Interim Memo 'The Use of Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' (Select Committee on School Facilities, Aug. 20, 2025) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf; https://wyoleg.gov/InterimCommittee/2025/SSF-2025082009-03_TheUseofCooperativePurchasingforProcurement-2.pdf)

renewalMechanics.value: N/A — Statute silent. No Wyoming statute or WDE administrative rule prescribes renewal mechanics (one-year extensions, CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, evergreen / automatic renewal, mutual written agreement, agency approval, or board approval) for a school district's contract with a private student-transportation contractor. W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) is silent on renewal. W.S. § 21-13-320 addresses only state reimbursement for district transportation maintenance-and-operation expenditures and for school-bus / vehicle purchases, and does not establish renewal mechanics for transportation-services contracts. WDE Chapter 20 ('Rules for the Student Transportation Component within the Education Resource Block Grant Model,' eff. January 2021) sets reimbursable-expense categories, fleet-size and replacement-life-cycle rules, and a State Vehicle Bid Process (Section 8) — it does not prescribe contract-term, renewal, or extension mechanics for district contracts with private contractors. At the district policy level (e.g., Campbell County School District #1 Regulation 3320-R and Natrona County School District #1 Regulation 3200), individual districts treat renewals and extensions of service and maintenance agreements as a matter of district discretion within their own approval thresholds, not pursuant to any statutory mechanism.
renewalMechanics.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-110(a)(viii); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-320; WDE Chapter 20 Rules (Student Transportation Component within the Education Resource Block Grant Model, eff. Jan. 2021) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf; https://edu.wyoming.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Chapter-20-Department-of-Education-Rules.pdf)

finalFields.statutoryCitation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) (Duties of boards of trustees — competitive bidding for purchases of insurance, supplies or materials; thresholds and publication; cooperative-educational-services 'goods or services' clause) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-320 (Student transportation; reimbursement formula; WDE rule-making and state vehicle-acquisition base-price competitive bidding) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-126 (Liability insurance on vehicles contracted to the school district) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-131 (School bus standards; operators; vehicle operation; liability limited) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf); Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-2-501 and § 21-2-502 (special-education entitlement and district contracting authorization) and § 21-13-321 (special-education reimbursement) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf); Wyo. Stat. Ann. tit. 16 ch. 6 (Public Works and Contracts; Articles 1-10) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title16.pdf); WDE Chapter 20 (https://edu.wyoming.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Chapter-20-Department-of-Education-Rules.pdf); Wyoming Legislative Service Office, 'The Use of Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' (Aug. 20, 2025) (https://wyoleg.gov/InterimCommittee/2025/SSF-2025082009-03_TheUseofCooperativePurchasingforProcurement-2.pdf). Wyoming statutes are silent on a maximum contract term, a transportation-services-specific rebid requirement at term-end, and renewal mechanics for district contracts with private student-transportation contractors.

districtVariance.label: Unknown
districtVariance.explanation: Because state law is silent on contract term, transportation-services-specific rebid at term-end, and renewal mechanics, individual school districts necessarily make these calls under their own purchasing policies. Two district policies were reviewed: Campbell County School District #1 Regulation 3320-R ('Purchasing') and Natrona County School District #1 Regulation 3200 ('Administrative Regulations — Purchasing Procedures'). Both mirror the statutory $25,000 / $50,000 thresholds in W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii). CCSD #1 authorizes 'Cooperative agreements ... in lieu of bidding as long as they are in compliance with Wyoming Statutes and Federal Regulations' and exempts 'renewals or extensions for maintenance or service agreements ... in an amount less than $25,000' from board approval. NCSD #1 generally restricts 'Online subscriptions and electronic software license agreements' to a 12-month period unless a non-appropriation, termination-for-cause, or termination-for-convenience clause is in the contract — a clause that is specific to subscriptions/licenses, not transportation. Neither district policy imposes a contract-term cap on transportation-services contracts. No official statewide WDE guidance, Wyoming Attorney General opinion, state-auditor report, or Wyoming School Boards Association guidance interpreting W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) uniformly across districts for transportation-services contracts was located. Because the statute itself does not set a term or mandate transportation-services rebidding, the variance question is largely moot at the statutory level (each district has discretion), but no evidence either way of uniform interpretation or of conflicting interpretation across districts of a state-law rule was found.

specialEducation.detail: No separate Wyoming statute or WDE administrative rule sets different contract term limits, rebid requirements, procurement rules, or renewal mechanics for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento transportation, foster-care transportation, alternative student transportation, or vendors serving students with disabilities. W.S. § 21-2-501 entitles 'Every child of school age in the state of Wyoming having a mental, physical or psychological disability which impairs learning ... to a free and appropriate education,' and W.S. § 21-2-502 provides that '[i]f the school district is unable to provide the necessary and appropriate programs and services, it shall contract with another school district or agency to obtain them' — a general inter-agency contracting authorization, not a procurement-rules carve-out. (The Stage 1 pack attributed this 'shall contract' language to '21-2-501 et seq.'; the specific quote sits in 21-2-502, which is part of the 21-2-501 et seq. article — verified.) W.S. § 21-13-321 governs state reimbursement of special-education programs and services (which may include related transportation) at one hundred percent (100%) of the amount actually expended in the prior year, but does not establish separate procurement, term, or rebid rules. Federal IDEA (20 U.S.C. § 1400 et seq.) establishes the duty to provide transportation as a related service under an IEP; it does not modify Wyoming contract or procurement rules. WDE Chapter 20 (the student-transportation reimbursement rule) does not differentiate procurement, term, or renewal mechanics for special-education transportation.

perFieldVerdicts.note: Re-verification of the full text of W.S. § 21-3-110, W.S. § 21-13-320, W.S. § 21-3-126, W.S. § 21-3-131, and Title 16 chapter 6 in the official LSO compilation confirmed Stage 1's negative finding: no Wyoming statute imposes a maximum term on a school district's transportation-services contract with a private contractor. Disconfirming searches for 'term,' 'duration,' 'years,' 'not exceed,' 'contract period,' 'extension,' and 'renewal' across Title 21 and Title 16 chapter 6 returned no transportation-contract term cap. The 2025 LSO cooperative-purchasing memo confirms the absence of any such cap.

The verbatim text of W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) was re-read in the official LSO Title 21 compilation and matches Stage 1's quotation. The 'Conditional' label is supported because the statute's bidding requirement on its face reaches 'insurance, supplies or materials' (not transportation services as such), but the same paragraph's BOCES clause refers to 'goods or services that are subject to this paragraph,' creating textual ambiguity. The 2025 LSO memo treats the 21-3-110 process as the operative school-district bidding statute and acknowledges that the prescriptive statutory process creates 'legal uncertainty' regarding alternative procurement methods (e.g., cooperative purchasing). No statute or AG opinion was located that mandates rebid at the end of a transportation-services contract term.

Re-verification confirmed that W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii), W.S. § 21-13-320, and WDE Chapter 20 are all silent on renewal mechanics for transportation-services contracts. The 2019 WDE memo to the Joint Education Interim Committee further confirms that 'Statutory changes may be necessary to authorize alternative transportation contracts,' reinforcing the absence of statutory renewal mechanics.

Each cited section was re-opened in the official LSO compilation of Title 21 and Title 16 and verified verbatim. Adjustment from Stage 1: the special-education 'shall contract with another school district or agency' language is verified at W.S. § 21-2-502 (a section within the 21-2-501 et seq. article), and the citation now identifies both 21-2-501 and 21-2-502 for clarity. All other Stage 1 citations were re-verified.

A second district policy (Natrona County SD #1, Regulation 3200) was added alongside Campbell County SD #1 Regulation 3320-R. Both mirror the 21-3-110(a)(viii) thresholds and neither sets a transportation-services contract-term cap. No state-wide guidance interpreting 21-3-110(a)(viii) uniformly was located; 'Unknown' label preserved because no evidence either way of state-wide uniform interpretation or of cross-district conflict of statutory interpretation was found.

Re-verification of W.S. § 21-2-501, § 21-2-502, § 21-13-321, and WDE Chapter 20 confirms no separate special-education procurement, term, or renewal rule for transportation contracts. The 'shall contract' language was located in 21-2-502 (within the 21-2-501 et seq. article), as the Stage 1 pack's citation form ('21-2-501 et seq.') properly contemplates.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: 'No statutory maximum found.'
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Wyoming may impose a maximum term elsewhere — e.g., a general local-government services-contract term cap in Title 16, a Title 9 procurement provision applicable to school districts, or a transportation-specific provision elsewhere in Title 21.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched the official LSO Title 21 PDF and Title 16 PDF for 'term,' 'duration,' 'years,' 'not exceed,' 'contract period,' 'multi-year,' 'extension,' 'renewal,' 'transportation contract,' 'pupil transportation,' 'school bus contract.' Reviewed Title 16 chapter 6 Articles 1-10 (public works/construction, resident-labor and Wyoming-materials preferences, public printing, life-cycle costs, accessibility, building plans/fire-marshal review, agency-account payments, construction contracts/alternate delivery, works of art, capital construction restrictions); none addresses a maximum term for school-district services contracts. Cross-checked the 2025 LSO 'Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' memo, which identifies only 9-2-3006 and 21-3-110 as the relevant procurement statutes.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority located. Negative finding holds.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: (a) A different statute mandates rebid at the end of a transportation-services contract term; (b) the 21-3-110(a)(viii) 'goods or services' BOCES clause extends the bidding requirement to transportation services more broadly than Stage 1 treats it; (c) WDE rule, AG opinion, or auditor guidance mandates rebid at term-end.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read 21-3-110(a)(viii) verbatim — confirmed 'insurance, supplies or materials' framing and the BOCES 'goods or services' clause. Searched WDE Chapter 20, the 2019 WDE alternative-contracts memo, the 2025 LSO cooperative-purchasing memo, the Wyoming AG opinion index (Justia/state-search), and the state auditor reports. No statute, rule, or opinion was located mandating rebid at the end of a transportation-services contract term. The 'goods or services' BOCES clause introduces interpretive ambiguity acknowledged in the rule statement; no Wyoming AG opinion or court decision resolving the question was located.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authoritative source rejecting 'Conditional.' Interpretive ambiguity disclosed within the rule statement.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirmed; confidence held at High overall but with the documented interpretive question on transportation-services scope of 21-3-110(a)(viii) carried into Exceptions / Unresolved Issues.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: (a) A statute or WDE rule prescribes one-year extensions, CPI / fuel escalators, evergreen renewal, or board / state approval for transportation contracts; (b) a Title 16 chapter 6 general-local-government provision addresses renewal of services contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched 21-3-110(a)(viii), 21-13-320, 21-13-321, Title 16 chapter 6, and WDE Chapter 20 for 'renewal,' 'extension,' 'option,' 'CPI,' 'fuel escalator,' 'evergreen,' 'roll over,' 'automatic renewal,' 'mutual written agreement,' 'agency approval,' 'board approval' as applied to transportation. Re-read 2019 WDE memo to Joint Education Committee — confirmed that 'Statutory changes may be necessary to authorize alternative transportation contracts,' reinforcing statutory silence.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority located. Negative finding holds.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate Wyoming statute or WDE rule sets different procurement, term, rebid, or renewal rules for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or vendors serving students with disabilities.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read W.S. § 21-2-501 (entitlement to FAPE for children with disabilities), § 21-2-502 (district duty to provide programs/services; inter-district contracting authorization), § 21-13-321 (special-education reimbursement). Verified that the 'shall contract with another school district or agency' language is at § 21-2-502 (the Stage 1 pack's '21-2-501 et seq.' citation form contemplates this section). Searched WDE Chapter 20 and WDE special-education rules for special-ed-specific procurement / term / renewal rules. No separate rule located. Federal IDEA addresses the duty to provide transportation as a related service but does not modify state contract/procurement rules.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority located. Citation form adjusted to identify §§ 21-2-501 and 21-2-502.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirmed; no downgrade. Minor citation refinement.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statewide WDE guidance, an AG opinion, an auditor report, or WSBA guidance interprets 21-3-110(a)(viii) uniformly across districts for transportation-services contracts; or two district policies treat statutory terms in materially conflicting ways.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Reviewed Campbell County SD #1 Regulation 3320-R 'Purchasing' and Natrona County SD #1 Regulation 3200 'Administrative Regulations — Purchasing Procedures.' Both mirror the 21-3-110(a)(viii) thresholds; neither sets a transportation-services contract-term cap; CCSD #1 has explicit exemptions for renewals/extensions of maintenance/service agreements under $25,000 from board approval; NCSD #1 generally caps online subscriptions/license agreements at 12 months absent specified termination clauses. Searched WDE bulletins, Wyoming auditor reports, Wyoming AG opinion index, and WSBA resources for state-wide interpretive guidance — none located.
challengeMatrix.result: No disconfirming authority located (no uniformity guidance and no documented cross-district conflict in statutory interpretation). 'Unknown' label preserved.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness: cited sections current and not superseded.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2024, 2025, or 2026 session law amended, repealed, renumbered, or superseded W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii), § 21-13-320, § 21-3-126, § 21-3-131, § 21-2-501 / § 21-2-502, or § 21-13-321 in a way that changes the answers.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read the LSO compilation Title 21 file as published; located references to 'Repealed by Laws 2025, ch. 24, § 2' in 21-3-110 paragraph (x)(A)(II) (a leased-facilities subprovision unrelated to transportation contracting) and to 'Repealed by Laws 2026, ch. 82, § 3' in 21-13-320 subparagraphs (g)(v)(B) and (h)(i) and the addition of subsection (o) for school year 2026-2027 reimbursement of remaining balances on vehicle purchases made before July 1, 2026. None of these amendments alter the maximum-term / rebid / renewal answers.
challengeMatrix.result: No material currentness conflict. Code-currency check documented.

sourcesReverified: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) — verbatim text re-read and matches Stage 1's quotation.

Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-320 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) — subsection (f) competitive-bidding-for-vehicle-base-price provision re-verified; 2026 amendments noted (Laws 2026, ch. 82, § 3) and confirmed not to alter answers.
Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-126 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) — re-verified; insurance pre-requisite for private-vehicle contracts confirmed.

Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-131 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) — re-verified; school-bus operator / vehicle / video-system standards confirmed; no procurement / contract-term provisions.

Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-2-501 and § 21-2-502 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) — re-verified; entitlement at 21-2-501, district duty to provide and contract for programs/services at 21-2-502.
Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-321 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) — re-verified; special-education reimbursement formula confirmed; no separate procurement / term / rebid rule.
Wyo. Stat. Ann. tit. 16 ch. 6 (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title16.pdf) — re-verified Articles 1-10; no general services-contract term cap applicable to school districts.

Wyoming Department of Education, Chapter 20 — Rules for the Student Transportation Component within the Education Resource Block Grant Model (https://edu.wyoming.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Chapter-20-Department-of-Education-Rules.pdf) — re-verified; reimbursable-expense, fleet, life-cycle, and State Vehicle Bid Process provisions confirmed; no contract-term, rebid, or renewal mechanics.

Wyoming Legislative Service Office, 'The Use of Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' (Select Committee on School Facilities, Aug. 20, 2025) (https://wyoleg.gov/InterimCommittee/2025/SSF-2025082009-03_TheUseofCooperativePurchasingforProcurement-2.pdf) — content re-verified via the markdown extract on file; identifies W.S. § 9-2-3006 and W.S. § 21-3-110 as the 'Conflicting Statutes' for public-entity procurement and confirms 21-3-110(a)(viii)'s 'insurance, supplies or materials' scope; proposes statutory amendments to authorize cooperative purchasing.

Wyoming Department of Education memo, 'Student Transportation Efficiencies and Alternative Contracts' (Joint Education Interim Committee, June 5-6, 2019) (https://wyoleg.gov/InterimCommittee/2019/04-201906056-02_WDETransportationMemo.pdf) — re-verified; WDE confirms that 'Statutory changes may be necessary to authorize alternative transportation contracts.'

Campbell County School District #1, Regulation 3320-R Purchasing (https://www.ccsd.k12.wy.us/post-details/~board/district-policies/post/3320-r-purchasing) — re-verified; thresholds mirror 21-3-110(a)(viii); explicit exemption for renewals/extensions of maintenance/service agreements under $25,000 from board approval; no transportation-services contract-term cap.

Natrona County School District #1, Regulation 3200 Admin Regs Purchasing Procedures (https://go.boarddocs.com/wy/ncsd1/Board.nsf/goto?open=&id=94WPJ963A063) — newly added in Stage 2; thresholds mirror 21-3-110(a)(viii); 12-month cap on online-subscriptions / electronic-software-license agreements (specific to subscriptions / licenses, not transportation); no transportation-services contract-term cap.
sourcesUnreachable: WebFetch of https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf surfaced the raw PDF binary; text extraction relied on the previously extracted markdown of the same official LSO compilation. The official URL is reachable and the file content was re-verified against the markdown extract, but a single WebFetch pass against the PDF binary did not itself render readable text.

WebFetch of https://wyoleg.gov/InterimCommittee/2025/SSF-2025082009-03_TheUseofCooperativePurchasingforProcurement-2.pdf returned a non-target document title in one pass (the file at that URL was extracted as 'WSBA Letter 4 (Aug. 19, 2025)' rather than the LSO memo). The substantive content of the LSO memo was re-verified against the previously extracted markdown of the same memo; the URL remains correct per the official LSO search result (top hit on the wyoleg.gov site for the exact memo title).

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) (Duties of boards of trustees)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2025 Wyoming Statutes as compiled by the Legislative Service Office; PDF reflects the official compilation in force in 2025, and includes references to 2025 and 2026 chapter laws.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: '(viii) Obtain competitive bids when any purchase of insurance, supplies or materials other than textbooks costing more than twenty-five thousand dollars ($25,000.00) and less than fifty thousand dollars ($50,000.00) is contemplated unless precluded by other regulation or statute. If the amount of the purchase of insurance, supplies or materials other than textbooks is equal to or exceeds fifty thousand dollars ($50,000.00), a call for bids shall be published at least once in a newspaper of general circulation in the district and on the state procurement website, as defined in W.S. 9-2-3001(b)(vii). For any contract for a capital construction project with an estimated value in excess of fifty thousand dollars ($50,000.00), the board shall publish a call for bids in a newspaper of general circulation in the district at least once each week for two (2) consecutive weeks. All contracts for capital construction projects shall be let in accordance with W.S. 9-2-3004 and 9-2-3006 except as provided in this paragraph. ... No contract shall be divided for the purpose of avoiding this paragraph. ... A district shall independently meet the requirements of this paragraph when procuring goods or services that are subject to this paragraph through a board of cooperative educational services.' — Statute speaks principally to insurance / supplies / materials and capital construction; sets no term cap and no renewal mechanism. The BOCES clause's 'goods or services' phrasing creates textual ambiguity as to the scope of 'subject to this paragraph.'

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-320 (Student transportation; reimbursement formula; WDE rule-making and competitive bidding for vehicle base prices)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2025 Wyoming Statutes (LSO compilation); includes 2026 amendments to (g)(v)(B), (h)(i), and addition of (o).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; Special-education differentiation
sourceLedger.relevantText: '(f) ... the department shall ... establish a base price for each school bus type or other student transportation vehicle type for the applicable fiscal period ... The department shall also establish a process including competitive bidding which guarantees the acquisition of school buses and other student transportation vehicles approved for reimbursement and complying with state minimum standards and district fleet size restrictions at the established base price for the applicable fiscal year. Department rules shall establish appropriate restrictions on how and under which conditions a school district may procure a school bus or other student transportation vehicle to ensure that the procurement method used is the most cost effective.' The competitive-bidding mandate runs to the State's vehicle base-price acquisition process for reimbursement — not to a district's contract with a private transportation contractor. No term cap, rebid, or renewal-mechanic provisions for transportation-services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-126 (Liability insurance on vehicles)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The board of trustees of any school district in the state of Wyoming or the owner of any vehicle contracted to the school district under the statutes of this state is hereby required to procure liability insurance covering any vehicle used for the transportation of school children or used in the operation of the school district. When private vehicles are contracted to the school district, the contract shall not be fully executed until the owner of the vehicle has filed with the school district the required insurance policy.' Acknowledges that districts may contract for transportation; imposes insurance prerequisite; no term, rebid, or renewal rules.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-3-131 (School bus standards; operators; vehicle operation; liability limited)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Sets minimum operator and operational standards: annual training of at least six (6) hours; two annual safety inspections (one by a non-district person); daily pre-trip inspections; emergency evacuation drills at least twice per school year; external-video-system equipment requirement for all buses transporting students to/from school and student activities (eff. school year 2016-2017). No procurement, contract-term, rebid, or renewal mechanics.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-2-501 (Children with disabilities entitled to free and appropriate education) and § 21-2-502 (Duties of school districts; interdistrict contracts; assistance of state superintendent)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 21-2-501: 'Every child of school age in the state of Wyoming having a mental, physical or psychological disability which impairs learning, shall be entitled to and shall receive a free and appropriate education in accordance with his capabilities.' 21-2-502: 'If the school district is unable to provide the necessary and appropriate programs and services, it shall contract with another school district or agency to obtain them. If the programs and services cannot reasonably be provided by the district or by interdistrict contracts, the state superintendent shall assist local boards of trustees in arranging for the appropriate educational programs and services either within or without the state pursuant to its rules and regulations and financed as provided by law.' Establishes the district duty and an inter-agency contracting authorization; no special-ed-specific procurement, term, or rebid rules for transportation-services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. § 21-13-321 (Special education; amount provided for special education programs and services; reimbursement for out-of-district placements; district reporting requirements; billing for Medicaid authorized school based services)
sourceLedger.relevantText: '(b) Except for expenditures for out-of-district special education placements, the amount provided to a school district for special education shall be equal to one hundred percent (100%) of the amount actually expended by the district during the previous school year for special education programs and services ...' Reimbursement formula; no separate procurement, term, or rebid rule for special-education transportation contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyo. Stat. Ann. tit. 16 ch. 6 (Public Works and Contracts; Articles 1-10)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics (negative result)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Title 16 chapter 6 governs 'public works' (construction, maintenance, and repair of public buildings/facilities/systems), Wyoming resident-contractor and Wyoming-materials preferences, public-printing contracts, life-cycle cost analyses, public-building plans/fire-marshal review, agency-account payments, alternate-delivery-method construction contracts (CMAR), works of art in public buildings, and capital-construction restrictions. No article addresses general services contracts of local governments or school districts; no provision sets a maximum term for local-government services contracts or for school-district transportation-services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyoming Department of Education, Chapter 20 — Rules for the Student Transportation Component within the Education Resource Block Grant Model
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Rules effective January 2021 per WDE publication; promulgated under W.S. 31-5-118, 21-13-320, and 21-4-401.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section 3 defines reimbursable transportation expenses (operation, maintenance, advertising, administrative, allowable vehicle purchases, communication, computer, equipment, field trip, insurance for student-transportation vehicles, purchased services, repair and maintenance, salary and benefits, etc.). Section 5 addresses vehicles purchased outright or via lease-purchase: 'the Department shall reimburse the low bid base price minus the trade-in value.' Section 6 sets fleet size and replacement-life-cycle requirements. Section 8: 'State Vehicle Bid Process. The specifications, pursuant to Chapter 2, for each type and size of vehicle will be used in a public procurement of student transportation vehicles. The bids received will be analyzed to determine which bids meet the specifications at the lowest responsible price.' No section prescribes contract term, rebid, or renewal mechanics for a district's contract with a private student-transportation contractor.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 4 - Official legislative-research-office guidance citing binding authority
sourceLedger.citation: Wyoming Legislative Service Office, 'The Use of Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' (Select Committee on School Facilities, Agenda Item 9; Aug. 20, 2025)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Memo dated 2025; quotes statutes in force at memo date.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Identifies W.S. § 9-2-3006 and W.S. § 21-3-110 as the 'Conflicting Statutes' governing public-entity procurement. Confirms 21-3-110(a)(viii)'s scope: 'insurance, supplies or materials other than textbooks.' Notes that the prescriptive statutory process creates 'legal uncertainty' for districts wishing to use cooperative purchasing and proposes statutory amendments to authorize cooperative / 'piggyback' procurement. Confirms that, as of 2025, Wyoming statutes do not expressly authorize cooperative purchasing as an alternative procurement path.

sourceLedger.citation: Wyoming Department of Education memo, 'Student Transportation Efficiencies and Alternative Contracts' (Joint Education Interim Committee, June 5-6, 2019)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Memo dated June 2019; references then-applicable statutes and rules. Confirms ongoing absence of statutory authority for alternative transportation contracts.
sourceLedger.relevantText: WDE proposes 'allowing districts to enter into a contract with a parent or legal guardian for student transportation expenses in lieu of school district-provided transportation' and states: 'Statutory changes may be necessary to authorize alternative transportation contracts.' Confirms that Wyoming's transportation-contract framework is largely undefined by statute beyond operational and vehicle standards.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 5 - District policy manual citing the relevant statute
sourceLedger.citation: Campbell County School District #1, Regulation 3320-R Purchasing
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current district policy as published (revised Aug. 20, 2024); cites W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) thresholds.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Mirrors 21-3-110 thresholds ($25,000 informal quotes; $50,000 formal bids); authorizes 'Cooperative agreements ... in lieu of bidding as long as they are in compliance with Wyoming Statutes and Federal Regulations'; provides that 'Board approval is required for contracts. Exceptions are renewals or extensions for maintenance or service agreements and print or electronic subscriptions in an amount less than $25,000.00.' Illustrates district-level treatment in the absence of state-law renewal/term rules.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 5 - District policy manual citing the relevant statute
sourceLedger.citation: Natrona County School District #1, Regulation 3200 Administrative Regulations — Purchasing Procedures
sourceLedger.relevantText: Mirrors 21-3-110 thresholds ($25,000 informal quotes; $50,000 formal bids). 'Online subscriptions and electronic software license agreements shall not be entered into for a period of more than 12 months. Online subscriptions and electronic software license agreements with a length of more than 12 months may be approved if a non-appropriation clause, a termination for cause, or a termination for convenience clause is specifically defined in the contract for purchase agreement.' This 12-month cap is specific to online subscriptions and software licenses, not transportation. No transportation-services contract-term cap.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Re-opened the official LSO Title 21 PDF (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf) and verified verbatim the text of W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii), § 21-13-320 (including subsections (b), (f), (g), (h), (n), (o)), § 21-3-126, § 21-3-131, § 21-2-501, § 21-2-502, and § 21-13-321 against the Stage 1 quotations; re-opened the official LSO Title 16 PDF (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title16.pdf) and walked Articles 1-10 of chapter 6 to confirm the negative result on a generally applicable services-contract term cap. Re-verified WDE Chapter 20 (https://edu.wyoming.gov/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Chapter-20-Department-of-Education-Rules.pdf), the LSO 2025 Cooperative Purchasing memo, and the WDE 2019 Alternative Contracts memo. Added a second district policy (Natrona County SD #1 Regulation 3200) alongside Campbell County SD #1 Regulation 3320-R to address district variance. Adversarial search terms: 'term,' 'duration,' 'years,' 'not exceed,' 'contract period,' 'multi-year,' 'extension,' 'option,' 'renewal,' 'evergreen,' 'roll over,' 'automatic renewal,' 'CPI,' 'fuel escalator,' 'mutual written agreement,' 'agency approval,' 'board approval,' 'rebid,' 'recompete,' 're-award,' 'expiration of the term,' 'sole source,' 'emergency procurement,' 'cooperative purchasing,' 'interlocal,' 'piggyback,' 'small purchase threshold,' 'pupil transportation,' 'school bus contract,' 'IEP transportation,' 'McKinney-Vento,' 'foster care transportation,' 'alternative transportation.' Pathfinder searches against Justia and FindLaw returned no different rule; official LSO compilation remains the operative authority.
verificationNotes.currentLawCheck: Official code source: Wyoming Legislative Service Office compilation, 2025 Wyoming Statutes (https://wyoleg.gov/statutes/compress/title21.pdf and title16.pdf); reflects laws in force in 2025 and shows 2025 amendments (e.g., 'Repealed by Laws 2025, ch. 24, § 2' in 21-3-110 paragraph (x)(A)(II)) and 2026 amendments (e.g., 'Repealed by Laws 2026, ch. 82, § 3' in 21-13-320 subparagraphs (g)(v)(B) and (h)(i), plus the addition of 21-13-320(o) for school year 2026-2027). None of the 2025 / 2026 amendments alters the maximum-term, rebid, or renewal-mechanic answers. Administrative code: WDE Chapter 20 rules, eff. January 2021; remains the operative WDE student-transportation rule; no later WDE Chapter 20 revision was located.
verificationNotes.unresolvedIssues: Whether W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii)'s competitive-bidding requirement reaches a school district's contract for student-transportation services — i.e., whether 'insurance, supplies or materials' (the operative bidding terms) is augmented by the same paragraph's BOCES clause referring to 'goods or services that are subject to this paragraph' such that 'services' is treated as within the scope of the paragraph — is textually ambiguous. No Wyoming attorney-general opinion, court decision, WDE rule, or auditor report resolving the question was located. The reconciled answer treats the bidding requirement as facially scoped to insurance / supplies / materials (consistent with the LSO 2025 memo's framing), but the interpretive question is surfaced in the rule statement and Exceptions section.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No conflict between authoritative sources was located. The 2025 LSO cooperative-purchasing memo, the 2019 WDE alternative-contracts memo, WDE Chapter 20, and the two district policy manuals reviewed are all consistent in treating the operative statutes (21-3-110 and 21-13-320) as not setting transportation-services contract term, rebid, or renewal mechanics. The interpretive question on the scope of 21-3-110(a)(viii) is not a conflict between sources but an unresolved textual ambiguity within a single statute.

notesSpecialProvisions: Wyoming's transportation-contract framework is materially shaped by what it does not address. The operative school-district procurement statute, W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii), establishes only dollar-threshold-driven competitive bidding for 'insurance, supplies or materials' and a separate publication regime for capital-construction contracts; it sets no transportation-services-specific contract-term cap, no rebid requirement tied to the end of a transportation-services term, and no renewal mechanics. Small-purchase / dollar-threshold exemptions: the statute treats purchases below $25,000 as outside the bidding requirement and purchases between $25,000 and $50,000 as triggering competitive bids without the formal publication regime; at or above $50,000 the call for bids must be published in a newspaper of general circulation and on the state procurement website (as defined in W.S. § 9-2-3001(b)(vii)), and the contract may not be divided to avoid the requirement. Sole-source / emergency procurement: Wyoming statutes do not provide a unified sole-source / emergency-procurement framework for school-district services contracts (in contrast to the state-agency framework that operates around W.S. § 9-2-3001 et seq.); the 2025 LSO cooperative-purchasing memo confirms there is no express statutory authority for districts to use cooperative-purchasing / piggyback procurement and proposes legislative amendments to authorize it. Charter-school rules: charter-school transportation arrangements remain subject to general district transportation responsibilities under W.S. § 21-13-320 and to district policy; no separate transportation-contract term, rebid, or renewal rule for charter schools was located. Special-education differences: none in transportation contracting — W.S. §§ 21-2-501 / 21-2-502 entitle children with disabilities to FAPE and authorize a district unable to provide programs / services to 'contract with another school district or agency' to obtain them; W.S. § 21-13-321 funds special-education programs and services at one hundred percent (100%) of the prior year's actual expenditure; neither establishes a separate procurement, term, or renewal rule for special-education transportation contracts. State-approval requirements: WDE approval applies to vehicle purchases for reimbursement (Chapter 20 and W.S. § 21-13-320(f)–(g)) and to the State Vehicle Bid Process at the WDE / Department of Administration & Information level, but does not extend to approval of district contracts with private transportation contractors. Practical application of the five fields: because the operative statute is silent on term, rebid at term-end, and renewal mechanics for transportation services, individual districts make these calls under their own purchasing policies, which mirror the 21-3-110(a)(viii) dollar thresholds but vary in their treatment of renewals and extensions (CCSD #1 exempts renewals/extensions of maintenance/service agreements under $25,000 from board approval; NCSD #1 caps online subscriptions/license agreements at 12 months absent specified termination clauses, but does not impose a transportation-services term cap). Statutory interpretive question: the BOCES clause in 21-3-110(a)(viii) refers to 'goods or services that are subject to this paragraph,' which raises an unresolved textual question about whether the bidding requirement reaches services (including transportation services) more broadly than 'insurance, supplies or materials'; no Wyoming AG opinion or court decision resolves the question.
exceptionsUnresolved: Wyoming has one substantive unresolved issue. The interpretive scope of W.S. § 21-3-110(a)(viii) — whether its competitive-bidding requirement reaches a school district's contract for student-transportation services (i.e., whether 'insurance, supplies or materials' (the operative bidding terms) is augmented by the same paragraph's BOCES clause referring to 'goods or services that are subject to this paragraph') — is textually ambiguous. Searched: the verbatim text of 21-3-110(a)(viii) in the official LSO Title 21 PDF; the 2025 LSO 'Use of Cooperative Purchasing for Procurement' memo; WDE Chapter 20; the 2019 WDE alternative-contracts memo; the Wyoming AG opinion index; Wyoming auditor reports; WSBA resources. Disconfirming searches attempted: a Wyoming AG opinion construing 'services' in 21-3-110(a)(viii); a Wyoming court decision construing the scope of 21-3-110(a)(viii); a WDE rule extending 21-3-110(a)(viii) to transportation services. No resolving authority located. The reconciled answer treats the bidding requirement as facially limited to insurance / supplies / materials (consistent with the LSO 2025 memo) and identifies the question as open. All five required fields are populated for Wyoming with documented citations; no other field is unresolved.
